economic_notes: GBEs include Hatchet Lake Denesuline First Nation Economic Development Corporation (100%), Hatchet Lake Development Limited Partnership (99.99%), Waterfound Development LP (33.33%), Waterfound Development Corp (33.33%), and 102147914 Saskatchewan Ltd (49%). New investment in 102147914 Saskatchewan Ltd to purchase property in Prince Albert to be renovated and leased to Spruce Lodge, scheduled for occupancy October 2024.
notes: Five GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis including Hatchet Lake Development LP (99.99%), HLDFNEDC (100%), Waterfound Development LP (33.33%), Waterfound Development Corp (33.33%), and 102147914 Saskatchewan Ltd (49%). Significant increase in accounts receivable driven largely by ISC Roads & Bridges seasonal road receivable of $2.75M and PAGC receivable of $3.33M. Deferred revenue increased substantially from $18.6M to $24.7M. Financial statements signed May 2, 2025 — approximately 13 months after fiscal year end. Restricted cash includes capital projects ($8,020,754) and CMHC reserves ($870,575). Long-term debt includes CMHC mortgages ($5,273,434) and long-term debt ($24,158). Total government transfers calculated as ISC + CMHC + PAGC + FNT + FSIN + ADEA + Government of Saskatchewan + NLCDC + Northern Sport = 32,263,019 + 635,365 + 4,712,487 + 939,729 + 0 + 184,856 + 0 + 207,300 + 84,300 = 39,027,056; however net deferred revenue treatment means OSR calculation is complex — OSR estimated as user fees + rental + IBA + other revenue = 6,349 + 256,045 + 173,847 + 1,468,515 = 1,904,756 plus equity pickup 4,133,277 = 6,038,033 less deferred adjustments not applicable; deferred revenue lines netted in revenue make precise OSR/government transfer split difficult.
